content.full_text:
July 1, 1994

W.E. Sturk
Treasurer
Committee to Elect Tom Kidwell Assessor
503 North D Street
Madera, CA 93638

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-94-200

Dear Mr. Sturk:

This letter confirms the telephone advice this office provided to you on May 26, 1994.

The gift of "oriental pajamas" made to candidate Kidwell's wife is not a reportable contribution under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Under the Act, a contribution is defined as a payment, forgiveness of a loan, or an enforceable promise to make a payment, except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is received, unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for political purposes. (Section 82015.) Mr. and Mrs. Hill presented a personal gift to Mr. Kidwell's wife after both Mr. Kidwell and his wife visited the Hill home to solicit support for a political campaign. Despite the original purpose of the visit, it is apparent that the gift was a personal gift to the candidate's wife and was not made for a political purpose.

A gift given directly to members of an official's family are not gifts to the official unless used or disposed of by the official or at the official's discretion. (Regulation 18944.) As the gift was made to Mr. Kidwell's wife, and as the nature of the gift precludes its use by Mr. Kidwell, the gift was not made to Mr. Kidwell for purposes of the Act. Accordingly, if Mr. Kidwell is required to file a statement of economic interests as a county official, the Hills' gift to his wife need not be disclosed.

___________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5560

File No. A-94-200
Page 2


We trust that this letter fully addresses the questions
raised in your telephone request and your follow-up letter of
June 9, 1994. Should you have additional questions about your
obligations and responsibilities under the Act, this office
remains available to assist you.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Steven G. Churchwell
                                   General Counsel
                                   [signature]
                                   By:  Daniel E. Muallem
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: The gift of "oriental pajamas" made to candidate Kidwell's wife is not a reportable contribution under the Political Reform Act. As the gift was made to Mr. Kidwell's wife and the nature of the gift precludes its use by Mr. Kidwell, the gift was not made to Mr. Kidwell for purposes of the Act. Accordingly, if Mr. Kidwell is required to file a statement of economic interests as a county official, the Hills' gift to his wife need not be disclosed.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a gift of oriental pajamas made to candidate Tom Kidwell's wife by the Hills constitutes a reportable contribution under the Political Reform Act, and whether it must be disclosed on a statement of economic interests.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the gift of oriental pajamas to the candidate's wife is not a reportable contribution because it was a personal gift not made for political purposes, and gifts to family members are not attributed to the official unless used or disposed of at the official's discretion. Therefore, the gift does not require disclosure on any statement of economic interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear question and conclusion content in letter format. Telephone advice confirmation dated May 26, 1994 with follow-up letter dated June 9, 1994.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a gift of oriental pajamas made to candidate Tom Kidwell's wife by the Hills constitutes a reportable contribution under the Political Reform Act, and whether it must be disclosed on a statement of economic interests.

CONCLUSION: The gift of "oriental pajamas" made to candidate Kidwell's wife is not a reportable contribution under the Political Reform Act. As the gift was made to Mr. Kidwell's wife and the nature of the gift precludes its use by Mr. Kidwell, the gift was not made to Mr. Kidwell for purposes of the Act. Accordingly, if Mr. Kidwell is required to file a statement of economic interests as a county official, the Hills' gift to his wife need not be disclosed.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming telephone advice that a gift of oriental pajamas given to candidate Tom Kidwell's wife is not a reportable political contribution under the Political Reform Act because it was a personal gift not made for political purposes. The gift does not require disclosure on any statement of economic interests.
